speaker: HELICANUS
text: No, Escanes, know this of me:

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Antiochus from incest lived not free,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: For which the most high gods not minding longer

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To withhold the vengeance that they had in store

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Due to this heinous capital offense,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Even in the height and pride of all his glory,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: When he was seated in a chariot of

speaker: HELICANUS
text: An inestimable value, and his daughter with him,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: A fire from heaven came and shriveled up

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Those bodies even to loathing, for they so stunk

speaker: HELICANUS
text: That all those eyes adored them, ere their fall,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Scorn now their hand should give them burial.

speaker: ESCANES
text: ’Twas very strange.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And yet but justice; for though this king were great,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: His greatness was no guard to bar heaven’s shaft,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But sin had his reward.

speaker: ESCANES
text: ’Tis very true.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: See, not a man in private conference

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Or counsel has respect with him but he.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: It shall no longer grieve without reproof.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              LORD
text: And cursed be he that will not second it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Follow me, then.—Lord Helicane, a word.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: With me? And welcome. Happy day, my lords.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Know that our griefs are risen to the top,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And now at length they overflow their banks.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Your griefs? For what? Wrong not your prince you love.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Wrong not yourself, then, noble Helicane.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: But if the Prince do live, let us salute him,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Or know what ground’s made happy by his breath.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: If in the world he live, we’ll seek him out;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: If in his grave he rest, we’ll find him there,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And be resolved he lives to govern us,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Or dead, give ’s cause to mourn his funeral

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And leave us to our free election.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Whose death’s indeed the strongest in our censure;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: And knowing this kingdom is without a head—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Like goodly buildings left without a roof

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Soon fall to ruin—your noble self,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: That best know how to rule and how to reign,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: We thus submit unto, our sovereign.

speaker: ALL
text: Live, noble Helicane!

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Try honor’s cause; forbear your suffrages.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: If that you love Prince Pericles, forbear.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Take I your wish, I leap into the seas,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Where’s hourly trouble for a minute’s ease.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: A twelve-month longer let me entreat you

speaker: HELICANUS
text: To forbear the absence of your king;

speaker: HELICANUS
text: If in which time expired, he not return,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: I shall with agèd patience bear your yoke.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: But if I cannot win you to this love,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Go search like nobles, like noble subjects,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: And in your search spend your adventurous worth,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Whom if you find and win unto return,

speaker: HELICANUS
text: You shall like diamonds sit about his crown.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: To wisdom he’s a fool that will not yield.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And since Lord Helicane enjoineth us,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: We with our travels will endeavor.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: Then you love us, we you, and we’ll clasp hands.

speaker: HELICANUS
text: When peers thus knit, a kingdom ever stands.
